summary: Gate or remove Replit-specific plugins for non-Replit environments
problem_statement: The project includes Replit-specific plugins and configuration from its MVP development environment. These plugins add unnecessary overhead, cause warnings or errors in non-Replit environments, and confuse developers who are not running on Replit. They should be gated behind environment detection or removed entirely if no longer needed.
in_scope: Identify all Replit-specific plugins, dependencies, and configuration in the project
Gate Replit-specific plugins behind an environment check (e.g. process.env.REPL_ID or equivalent) so they only load on Replit
Alternatively, remove Replit-specific plugins entirely if the project is no longer deployed on Replit
Verify the application builds and runs correctly in both Replit and non-Replit environments after changes
out_of_scope: Migrating away from Replit as a deployment platform — this is about plugin compatibility only
Adding new plugins or replacing Replit plugins with alternatives
Changing the development workflow beyond plugin gating
requirements: Replit-specific plugins do not load, warn, or error in non-Replit environments
If gated rather than removed, plugins still function correctly on Replit
The application builds and starts without warnings from Replit plugins in non-Replit environments
acceptance_criteria: Replit-specific plugins are gated behind environment checks or removed
No Replit-related warnings or errors appear when running outside Replit
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
The application builds and runs correctly in non-Replit environments
dependencies: P8-R2-26 — unused dependency audit may overlap with Replit plugin removal
risks: If the project is still actively developed on Replit, removing plugins outright may break that workflow — prefer gating over removal unless Replit is confirmed abandoned
implementation_notes: Check vite.config.ts and package.json for Replit-specific entries (e.g. @replit/vite-plugin-*, replit-related dev dependencies). Use dynamic imports or conditional plugin arrays in Vite config to gate loading. Example: plugins: [...(process.env.REPL_ID ? [replitPlugin()] : [])].
